sprintGoal: Port Brain MCP's 2-phase cross-context retrieval into memclaw_recall. Phase 1 = current hybrid search (unchanged). Phase 2 = opt-in: re-runs the same query embedding without caller_agent_id so storage returns only scope_team/scope_org memories, applies a lower similarity threshold and a score discount, caps the blend at cc_ratio of total results, marks each Phase 2 hit with source_type='cross_context'. Goal: agents surface shared/infra knowledge that Phase 1 crowds out with higher-scoring own memories.

workItems.objective: Add source_type field to MemoryOut schema so Phase 2 retrieval provenance is visible to callers.
acceptanceCriteria: schemas.py MemoryOut has source_type: str | None = None field
Phase 2 hits carry source_type='cross_context' in API/MCP responses
Phase 1 hits carry source_type=None (no change to existing behaviour)

reasons: Additive field with None default — backwards compatible, existing callers unaffected

workItems.objective: Create CrossContextEnrich pipeline step that runs Phase 2 search and appends discounted shared-scope hits to filtered_rows.
acceptanceCriteria: When cross_context=False (default), step returns SKIPPED with zero effect on results

When cross_context=True, Phase 2 results exclude Phase 1 IDs, pass cc_threshold, have score multiplied by cc_discount, and total Phase 2 rows are capped at max(1, int((len_phase1 + len_cross) * cc_ratio))
Step is exported from steps/search/__init__.py and inserted after PostFilterResults in compositions/search.py

reasons: Step is opt-in (cross_context=False default); all existing search behaviour unchanged
Phase 2 uses existing sc.scored_search() — no new storage endpoints

payload.key_design: Phase 2 omits caller_agent_id → storage visibility filter returns scope_team + scope_org only. Python-side: exclude Phase 1 IDs, apply cc_threshold on vec_sim, multiply score by cc_discount, cap at cc_ratio.

workItems.objective: Wire cross_context and weight params through search_memories() → _search_memories_pipeline() → PipelineContext.data, and expose them on the memclaw_recall MCP tool.
acceptanceCriteria: search_memories() and _search_memories_pipeline() in memory_service.py accept cross_context, cc_top_m, cc_threshold, cc_ratio, cc_discount with Brain-MCP defaults
PipelineContext.data contains all five keys on every search invocation
memclaw_recall MCP tool exposes all five as optional Annotated params with descriptions
memclaw_recall passes all five to search_memories()

reasons: All new params default to off/Brain defaults — no behaviour change unless cross_context=True is passed

workItems.objective: Deploy to .53 and verify Phase 2 cross-context hits appear with source_type='cross_context' when cross_context=true.
acceptanceCriteria: docker compose build core-api succeeds on 192.168.1.53
docker compose up -d core-api brings container up healthy
memclaw_recall with cross_context=false returns Phase 1 results only (source_type=null for all)
memclaw_recall with cross_context=true, cc_threshold=0.1 returns at least one result with source_type='cross_context' (given a scope_org or scope_team memory exists)

reasons: Restarts core-api container on production host — brief downtime during restart

payload.deploy_command: ssh 192.168.1.53 "cd ~/dev/caura-memclaw && git pull && docker compose build core-api && docker compose up -d core-api"
